facts: India has a floating post office! It is on a boat in a lake in Dal, Srinagar.
The game of Snakes and Ladders started in India a long time ago to teach kids about good and bad.
India is the first place where people found and mined diamonds in the ground.
China has the longest bridge in the world. It is called the Danyang-Kunshan Grand Bridge and is 102 miles (164 kilometers) long!
In China, every year has an animal name, like the Year of the Tiger or Year of the Dragon.
China built a giant solar power plant shaped like a giant Panda bear that you can see from space!
The capital city of South Sudan is Juba.
In South Sudan, people speak 2 main official languages (English and Arabic), but there are over 60 other local languages used by different groups!
The capital city of Oman is Muscat.
In Oman, the main official language is Arabic, but people also use 3 or 4 other common languages like English and Baluchi.
The capital city of Sierra Leone is Freetown.
In Sierra Leone, English is the official language, but people use about 25 different languages to talk to each other every day!
The capital city of Liberia is Monrovia.
In Liberia, the official language is English, but there are over 20 other indigenous languages used across the country.
The capital city of the Central African Republic is Bangui.
In this country, people use 2 official languages: French and Sango.
The capital city of Palau is Ngerulmud.
In Palau, people use 2 official languages, Palauan and English, but some islands also use Japanese!
The capital city of Tuvalu is Funafuti.
In Tuvalu, people use 2 main languages: Tuvaluan and English.
The capital city of Yemen is Sana'a.
In Yemen, the official language is Arabic, though many people use different regional versions of it.
The capital city of Guinea-Bissau is Bissau.
In Guinea-Bissau, the official language is Portuguese, but almost everyone uses a language called Kriol to talk to their friends.
